query: What should a fresh agent trust before planning today's release?

memory_entries.title: Release runbook source
memory_entries.summary: Current deploy checks come from the reviewed release runbook.
memory_entries.safe_use: Use as trusted context for release-check sequencing.

memory_entries.title: Draft release-risk synthesis
memory_entries.summary: Generated synthesis says the worker queue is probably the riskiest area.
memory_entries.safe_use: Treat as a hypothesis and verify against source-backed context before acting.

memory_entries.title: Old staging rollback note
memory_entries.summary: Rollback note references the retired staging namespace.

source_trust.stale_reason: Namespace ownership changed after the note was captured.

memory_entries.safe_use: Do not follow this note until a current source-backed rollback path replaces it.

memory_entries.title: Security agent private finding
memory_entries.summary: A permitted cross-agent recall found a private finding, but details are policy-limited.
memory_entries.safe_use: Ask for an authorized summary instead of expanding private details.

safe_next_action: Plan from the source-backed runbook, verify the generated synthesis before using it, ignore the stale rollback note, and request an authorized summary for policy-limited private findings.
